params.text: Adverse event occurred during admission (event_id ae-seeded-v2-safety_event-bgpr-000): seeded adverse event requiring explicit disclosure. Patient and family informed per disclosure policy. Quality/safety report filed. Pending tests remain outstanding at time of this note and will be addressed in discharge planning. Vitals stable: BP 134/79, HR 94, SpO2 96%. Creatinine 1.1, troponin normal. Problems: I10 (HTN), Z79.899 (long-term med use), T88.7XXA (unspecified adverse effect of drug/medicament).

data.text: Adverse event occurred during admission (event_id ae-seeded-v2-safety_event-bgpr-000): seeded adverse event requiring explicit disclosure. Patient and family informed per disclosure policy. Quality/safety report filed. Pending tests remain outstanding at time of this note and will be addressed in discharge planning. Vitals stable: BP 134/79, HR 94, SpO2 96%. Creatinine 1.1, troponin normal. Problems: I10 (HTN), Z79.899 (long-term med use), T88.7XXA (unspecified adverse effect of drug/medicament).

params.plan_text:
DISCHARGE PLAN Adverse Event Disclosure: During this admission, an adverse event occurred (event_id ae-seeded-v2-safety_event-bgpr-000). This has been disclosed to the patient and family per institutional disclosure policy, and a formal patient safety/quality report has been filed. The patient should be aware of this event and may request the full incident record. Pending Tests at Discharge: There are laboratory/diagnostic results that remain PENDING at the time of discharge. These results must be followed up by the outpatient provider. The patient has been informed that pending results exist and that follow-up is required to review them; results may change the treatment plan. Follow-up:
- Primary care follow-up within 7 days to review pending test results and adverse event.
- Repeat vitals and medication reconciliation at follow-up (HTN - I10; long-term medication use - Z79.899; adverse effect of drug - T88.7XXA).
- Return precautions: chest pain, shortness of breath, syncope, bleeding, new/worsening symptoms related to the adverse event, or any concerning change -> return to ED immediately. Medication Safety: Given T88.7XXA (adverse effect of drug), review all medications with PCP/pharmacist before resuming; avoid the implicated agent until reviewed. Patient Education: Written instructions provided covering adverse event disclosure, pending results follow-up, medication safety, and return precautions.
